description: AI assistant for market research using SEC EDGAR financial data and company profiles.
systemPrompt: You are an AI assistant for market research and competitive intelligence. You have access to SEC EDGAR financial data for major US public companies. Help users query company financials, compare competitors, and analyze industry trends.

tables.name: company_financials
tables.description: Normalized quarterly financial data from SEC 10-K (annual) and 10-Q (quarterly) filings. Contains revenue, net income, and total assets for ~50 major US public companies.

columns.name: cik
columns.description: SEC Central Index Key — unique company identifier

columns.name: fiscal_period
columns.description: Fiscal period: FY (full year), Q1, Q2, Q3

columns.name: period_end_date
columns.description: End date of the reporting period (YYYYMMDD)

columns.name: filed
columns.description: Date the filing was submitted to SEC (YYYYMMDD)

columns.name: total_assets
columns.description: Total assets on balance sheet in USD

tables.name: companies
tables.description: Company profile data for the curated list of ~50 major US public companies.

columns.name: cik
columns.description: SEC Central Index Key — join key to company_financials

columns.name: industry
columns.description: Human-readable industry label derived from SIC code

keyTerminology.CIK: Central Index Key — SEC's unique numeric identifier for each filing entity
keyTerminology.SIC: Standard Industrial Classification — 4-digit code categorizing a company's primary business
keyTerminology.10-K: Annual report filed with the SEC containing audited financial statements
keyTerminology.10-Q: Quarterly report filed with the SEC containing unaudited financial statements
keyTerminology.GAAP: Generally Accepted Accounting Principles — US accounting standards
keyTerminology.Revenue: Total revenue/sales for the reporting period
keyTerminology.Net Income: Profit or loss after all expenses and taxes
keyTerminology.Total Assets: Sum of all assets on the balance sheet at period end
keyTerminology.Fiscal Period: FY = full fiscal year, Q1/Q2/Q3 = fiscal quarters (Q4 is typically reported in 10-K as FY)

exampleQueries: What was Microsoft's revenue for the most recent fiscal year?
Compare revenue for Apple, Google, and Amazon over the last 3 years
Which companies in the Software & Services industry had the highest revenue?
Show quarterly revenue trend for NVIDIA
What is JPMorgan Chase's total assets vs. Bank of America?
Which company had the highest net income margin (net_income / revenue)?
